name: Sick Sinus Syndrome
description: Sick sinus syndrome is a dysfunction of the heart's natural pacemaker (sinoatrial node), causing an abnormally slow heart rate, pauses, or alternating bradycardia and tachycardia. It leads to syncope and exercise intolerance.
treatment_overview: Medical management with antiarrhythmic drugs (atropine, theophylline) provides temporary relief. Permanent pacemaker implantation is the most effective treatment for persistent clinical signs.
prevention_tips: Annual cardiac Holter monitoring in at-risk breeds
